pages.raw_text:
A serious stumbling block has
been thrown in the way of imme-
date construction of the new Olive
st. bridge, the plans for which have
been approved by the board OF
works. For several years the
Northern Pacific railroad has al
lowed the county the use of 60 feet
along the south of its right-of-way
for the Trent road. This does not
belong to the county and there is
no agreement in writing between
the county and railroad officials.
With the building of the Napa st,
viaduct the Trent road was cut In
two at that point and if it is to be
continued in use it will be neces-
sary to bridge over the south end
of the viaduct.


The county commissioners refuse
to build such a bridge. They have
agreed, however. to pay $4,000 as
their share of the cost of the pro-
posed new olive ST. bridge. IhlS
sum. with the $4,000 the Traction
Co. has agreed to pay, will leave
about $4,000 as the city's expense.
Charles Liftchild, the principal
promoter of the Olive st. bridge
and a large property owner on
Olive St, who plans to erect bust
ness houses similar to those on
E. Sprague av., will probably en-
deavor to have the city build the
Trent road bridge if the county
commissioners do no, finally con-
sent to its being done at the
county's expense. Property in the
vicinity of the Olive st. bridge, it
is stated, has almost doubled in
value since the bridge work was
taken up.
